bead.title: BL (Boundary Lockdown): caller narrowing + relocate concretes to interna: part 2
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-8ea2e451 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-74452926 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-inter
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
bead.acceptance:
1. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
2. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"ac specifies a concrete, differentiated scope for 'part 2' distinct from its sibling child: This depth-2 child inherits the full TD-027 §21 problem/root-cause narrative from parent ddx-8ea2e451 (and its own parent ddx-74452926), but the decomposition step that produced this child never actually differentiates 'part 2 of 2' from its sibling. The parent's multi-item PROPOSED FIX is cut off mid-sentence ('use narrower sub-inter\\n...[truncated]'), losing whichever items (narrow-candidate sub-interfaces, the Backend/ReadOnlyBackend composite assembly, the compile-time assertion) were meant to distinguish this child from part 1. The child-specific PROPOSED FIX section that follows is pure boilerplate ('Implement only this child acceptance slice') backed by only generic gates (full test suite + lefthook), with no reference to a sibling bead ID, no enumerated deliverable, and no named Test* function. An executing agent cannot determine which concrete subset of caller-narrowing or interface-assembly work belongs to this bead versus its unnamed sibling. prior_attempts[0] (no_changes, agent exited without a commit) is consistent with this ambiguity — a prior agent found no boundable, actionable diff. prior_attempts[1] (execution_failed on a stale git index.lock) is unrelated infra noise and not a current blocker. Attempting execution as written risks either repeating the no-op outcome or silently claiming/overlapping scope that belongs to the sibling child, which is an unsafe scope choice rather than a simple prose cleanup.","fingerprint":"7b522899ac22ca7f31e16605c60694d0ee701bb31dce650d873608b544d71c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"7b522899ac22ca7f31e16605c60694d0ee701bb31dce650d873608b544d71c0c"}
